title: Add automated record-chase training and submission pipeline
body:
This PR adds end-to-end automation scripts for Parameter Golf:

- run_remote_fast.sh: parallel multi-candidate sweep
- run_remote_record_chase.sh: staged record-chase search (screen + finals)
- submit_remote_fast.sh: auto-build submission folder + commit/push/PR
- runpod_bootstrap_fast.sh: one-shot bootstrap on CUDA hosts

Goal: speed up reproducible SOTA chasing and submission workflow on RunPod/H100.

comments.body: `submit_remote_fast.sh` assumes it is run from the repo root (uses relative paths like `runs/...` and `records/...`) but never `cd`s into a known location. This is inconsistent with the other automation scripts (which `cd` to `REPO_DIR`) and makes failures like “candidate script not found” easy to trigger when invoked from another directory. Consider `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"` (or similar) early in the script.

`run_remote_record_chase.sh` calls `submit_remote_fast.sh` with `RUN_DIR="${OUTROOT}"`, but `submit_remote_fast.sh` requires `${RUN_DIR}/candidate_manifest.json`. This record-chase script never creates `candidate_manifest.json`, so AUTO_SUBMIT will reliably fail with “Missing candidate manifest”. Either emit a compatible manifest in `${OUTROOT}` (mapping finalists to their script + env) or teach `submit_remote_fast.sh` to handle record-chase outputs (e.g., infer script/env some other way).
```suggestion
  if [[ -f "${OUTROOT}/candidate_manifest.json" ]]; then
    export RUN_DIR="${OUTROOT}"
    bash submit_remote_fast.sh
  else
    echo "AUTO_SUBMIT=1 but ${OUTROOT}/candidate_manifest.json not found; skipping auto-submit."
  fi
```

`New-SubmissionFolder` writes `submission.json` with `val_loss` set to the mean of `val_bpb` values, and does not write a `val_bpb` field at all. This conflicts with the repo’s submission format (see existing `records/**/submission.json` files and README guidance requiring `val_bpb`) and will generate incorrect submission metadata. Write `val_bpb` (and optionally `val_loss` separately if available) instead of putting BPB into `val_loss`.

The header comment says `FORK_REMOTE` defaults to `origin`, but the script actually defaults it to `fork` (`FORK_REMOTE="${FORK_REMOTE:-fork}"`). This mismatch will confuse users and can cause `git push` failures if no `fork` remote exists. Update the comment or change the default to match.
```suggestion
FORK_REMOTE="${FORK_REMOTE:-origin}"
```

If there are no `runs/remote_fast_*` directories, `ls -dt runs/remote_fast_*` will exit non-zero and, with `set -e`, the script will abort with an unhelpful `ls: cannot access ...` message. Consider explicitly checking for matches (e.g., via `compgen -G`) and printing a clear error like “no remote_fast runs found”.
```suggestion
  if compgen -G "runs/remote_fast_*" > /dev/null; then
    RUN_DIR="$(ls -dt runs/remote_fast_* | head -n1)"
  else
    echo "No remote_fast runs found (expected directories matching runs/remote_fast_*)."
    exit 1
  fi
```

In Stage A parallel mode, each job is assigned a single GPU ID (`visible_gpus` is a single index), but `nproc_local` is set from `NPROC_PER_JOB` (defaulting to 1). If a user sets `NPROC_PER_JOB>1`, `torchrun --nproc_per_node=${nproc_local}` will try to spawn multiple ranks on a single visible GPU and will fail. Either clamp `J` to 1 when assigning a single GPU, or allocate `visible_gpus` as a comma-list with `J` GPUs per job.

When `-PrepareSubmission` is used, the defaults `SubmissionAuthor="YOUR_NAME"` and `SubmissionGithubId="YOUR_GITHUB_ID"` will produce a scaffold with placeholder identity fields unless the user overrides them. Consider validating these inputs (e.g., error if they still equal the placeholder strings) before generating `submission.json`, similar to how `submit_remote_fast.sh` requires `GITHUB_ID`.

The first embedded Python block reads `os.environ["RUN_DIR"]`, but `RUN_DIR` is only a shell variable here (not exported) and this Python invocation does not prefix env vars (unlike the later one). As written, this will raise a `KeyError: 'RUN_DIR'` unless the caller exported RUN_DIR beforehand. Export RUN_DIR before invoking Python, or pass it inline (`RUN_DIR=... python3 - ...`) for this first block as well.

This script runs two separate Python blocks to (1) create the submission folder and (2) recompute `SUBMISSION_DIR`. Because the folder name includes `datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')`, the recomputed path can differ from the created folder (e.g., if the date rolls over at midnight), causing `git add` to fail or to add the wrong path. Prefer capturing the printed output path from the *creating* Python block and reusing it, rather than recomputing.
```suggestion
records_root = pathlib.Path("records") / "track_10min_16mb"
suffix = f"_AutoSweep_{best['candidate']}"
candidates = [
    d for d in records_root.iterdir()
    if d.is_dir() and d.name.endswith(suffix)
]
if not candidates:
    raise SystemExit(f"No submission directory found matching '*{suffix}' under {records_root}")
submission_dir = sorted(candidates)[-1]
print(str(submission_dir))
```

`submission.json` is being written with `"val_loss": round(mean_bpb, 6)`, but `mean_bpb` is the bits-per-byte score, not the validation loss. Repo documentation and existing submissions require a `val_bpb` field (and typically `val_loss` is a different metric). This will generate invalid/misleading submission metadata. Write `val_bpb` (and optionally the true mean `val_loss` if available) instead of storing BPB under `val_loss`.
